feedback.text: This is a great act that tackles many important aspects of making the modern internet more fair for the common man, but I believe it forgets a critical aspect that is important in digital fairness that the Stop Killing Games initiative or Stop Destroying Videogames petition discusses. It would be great if the EU could look into extending the act to include thoughts and ideas such as "end of life plans" for games and that video games should "require no connections to the publisher after support ends" (quoted from the stop killing games website and the stop destroying videogames petition). Thank you.
